skill.name: clojure-review
skill.description: Review Metabase Clojure and ClojureScript changes against project style, testing, API, module, and documentation conventions.
skill.summary: Review Metabase Clojure pull requests for style, quality, tests, and project conventions.
skill.author: metabase

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many backtick, weak-crypto, hard-link, and reconnaissance patterns, but the reviewed file is a Markdown skill guide and those high-risk hits are false positives from prose or inline examples. The skill does legitimately instruct agents to run local Metabase lint, format, test, and REPL commands, and it may ask agents to apply fixes or commit changes, so it should publish with a medium risk warning. No evidence found of network access, credential exfiltration, hidden scripts, or prompt injection attempts.

medium_findings.title: Local Command Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends running local Metabase mage commands for linting, formatting, tests, readability checks, and REPL evaluation. This is legitimate for a code review skill, but it can execute project code and should be limited to trusted repositories.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command strings are explicit and relevant to the skill workflow. The commands are local project tooling, so the concern is controlled execution risk rather than malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Possible Repository Mutation During Review
medium_findings.description: The instructions allow the agent to apply obvious fixes and commit changes after lint issues are resolved. This is useful for review automation, but it can modify repository state and should require normal user oversight.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly instructs applying fixes and committing changes. There is no malicious intent, but the filesystem and git side effects are clear.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Detections Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer interpreted many Markdown inline-code spans and fenced examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The reviewed file is documentation for an AI skill, not executable Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is Markdown prose, checklists, or command examples. No Ruby source, shell interpolation, or hidden executable file was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-risk weak-cryptography alerts do not correspond to cryptographic API use. The cited lines contain prose, naming examples, TODO examples, or review comments.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines contain no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or related cryptographic implementation. The matches appear to be substring or documentation noise.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts point to style-guide language about declarations, legacy MBQL, shell escaping, and review comments. No host inventory, user enumeration, or environment probing was present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are ordinary review guidance. They do not request system discovery commands or collection of host details.

low_findings.title: Hard Link Detection Is A False Positive
low_findings.description: The filesystem alert points to a REPL example containing println and quoted Clojure data. It does not create hard links or manipulate filesystem links.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact line is a shell example for evaluating Clojure output. No link command or hard-link API appears there.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash-Based Project Tooling
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on local Bash commands for linting, formatting, tests, readability checks, and REPL calls. Marketplace users should run it only in repositories where executing project tooling is acceptable.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is clearly documented in command examples. The risk depends on the trust level of the checked-out repository and local tooling.

content.value_statement: Clojure pull requests can miss project-specific style, testing, and API rules. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through focused Metabase Clojure review checks.
actual_capabilities: Checks Clojure and ClojureScript naming, visibility, line length, and function-size conventions.
Reviews docstrings, Markdown references, TODO format, and public var documentation expectations.
Evaluates Metabase REST API endpoint conventions, schemas, parameter naming, side effects, and tests.
Guides use of Metabase lint, format, readability, test, and REPL commands.
Reviews module layout, MBQL boundaries, database model naming, Toucan usage, and driver conventions.
limitations: It is tailored to Metabase conventions, not general Clojure community style alone.
It depends on the available diff, repository files, and local project tooling.
It does not replace clj-kondo, automated tests, or security review.
Command execution and suggested fixes should be reviewed before use in untrusted repositories.

use_cases.title: Review Metabase Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Find style, API, module, documentation, and test issues before a Clojure pull request is merged.

use_cases.title: Prepare Contributions
use_cases.description: Check a Clojure or ClojureScript change against Metabase expectations before requesting human review.

use_cases.title: Audit Refactors
use_cases.description: Review larger refactors for naming, namespace boundaries, test structure, and project-specific review concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Review A Small Diff
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a focused change
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Metabase Clojure diff for style-guide violations. Only report issues that should become review comments.

prompt_templates.title: Check API Endpoint Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these endpoint changes for Metabase API conventions, schemas, parameter naming, side effects, and missing tests.

prompt_templates.title: Review Tests And Modules
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this ClojureScript and Clojure change for test organization, module layout, namespace patterns, and lint concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Run A Full Metabase Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this branch with the Metabase Clojure checklist. Use available lint, format, readability, and test results to prioritize findings.

output_examples.input: A pull request adds a public Clojure function without documentation.
output_examples.output: This public var needs a docstring that explains its purpose, inputs, and return value.

output_examples.input: A new API endpoint accepts snake case query parameters and has no response schema.
output: Query parameters should use kebab case in Metabase endpoints.
Add a response schema to the endpoint before merging.

output_examples.input: A test namespace adds one large test with several unrelated assertions.
output_examples.output: Split this into separate tests for each behavior so failures identify the broken case clearly.

best_practices: Provide the pull request diff and any relevant changed files before asking for review.
Run lint and test commands in a trusted checkout when local execution is needed.
Ask for actionable review comments instead of general approval or praise.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill as a general Clojure style authority outside Metabase projects.
Do not run project commands from untrusted repositories without sandboxing or review.
Do not post comments that only praise code for following the guide.

faq.question: What code does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews Clojure and ClojureScript changes, with emphasis on Metabase project conventions.

faq.question: Can it run lint and tests?
faq.answer: It can guide local Metabase lint, formatting, readability, test, and REPL commands when those tools are available.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted repositories?
faq.answer: Use caution. The skill includes local command execution guidance, so untrusted repositories should be sandboxed.

faq.question: Does it replace human review?
faq.answer: No. It helps find style and quality issues, but maintainers still need to judge correctness and product impact.

faq.question: Does it check security bugs?
faq.answer: It is mainly a style and quality review skill. Security review should be handled separately.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
